texto: ...but the air's flat and stale and the people half-hearted. There's nothing to do there. You can make love without trouble or meaning, or get mildly drunk, or extract second-hand emotions from the cinema, or put your mind to sleep on a dance-floor, or play bridge, or throw yourself in front of a train on the Underground. There are forty ways of escaping from consciousness. But I want something more exciting than that.
explicacao: Citação de Eric Linklater sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “...but the air's flat and stale and the people half-hearted. There's nothing to do there. You can…” articula reflexao, eric-linklater, exciting em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como dia-chuvoso e exciting.

frase_original: ...but the air's flat and stale and the people half-hearted. There's nothing to do there. You can make love without trouble or meaning, or get mildly drunk, or extract second-hand emotions from the cinema, or put your mind to sleep on a dance-floor, or play bridge, or throw yourself in front of a train on the Underground. There are forty ways of escaping from consciousness. But I want something more exciting than that.
